source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: A number line goes 10, 20, 30, 40. What number is halfway between 20 and 30?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Gives 15 or 35 instead of finding the midpoint between the two marked values.

cognitive_load.justification: Simple midpoint check on an evenly spaced integer number line.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Which number would be further to the right on a number line?
options: They are in the same place

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Selects the smaller number, showing the number line direction is insecure.

cognitive_load.justification: Short recognition check of integer order on a number line.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: A number line shows 100 and 150 as neighbouring marks. Estimate the value halfway between them.

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Adds or subtracts 50 instead of halving the interval.

cognitive_load.justification: Uses interval size to estimate a midpoint on a larger integer scale.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: A number line starts at 0 and ends at 100. Where would 70 go?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places 70 at the midpoint instead of seven-tenths of the way across.

cognitive_load.justification: Requires verbal placement reasoning on a scaled line rather than choosing from options.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: What is the midpoint between 2990 and 3010 on a number line?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Gives 3001 or 3020 by miscalculating the halfway interval.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item centred on midpoint reasoning with large integers.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Rounds up to 140 without checking which multiple of 10 is closer.

cognitive_load.justification: Short nearest-10 rounding check using a number from the source examples.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Rounds down to 2150 by ignoring the ones digit.

cognitive_load.justification: Extends nearest-10 rounding to a four-digit integer.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Rounds up to 580 by treating any 4 as a round-up digit.

cognitive_load.justification: Nearest-10 rounding with a mid-hundreds number where the ones digit drives the decision.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Rounds to 1000 by anchoring to the lower thousand instead of the nearest one.

cognitive_load.justification: Increases the rounding unit to 1000 while keeping the structure familiar.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Rounds to 70 because the tens digit is 7 and the decimal part is ignored.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item that bridges integer rounding and decimal values rounded to a whole multiple of 10.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: What is the midpoint between 1.2 and 1.4 on a number line?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Gives 1.24 or 1.34 instead of finding the midpoint between the two decimal values.

cognitive_load.justification: Simple decimal midpoint check before full number-line estimation.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: A decimal lies halfway between 0.3 and 0.4 on a number line. What is it?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Gives 0.34 or 0.45, showing the interval has not been halved correctly.

cognitive_load.justification: Uses decimal intervals and midpoint reasoning together.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Which number would be between 0.8 and 0.9 on a number line?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Chooses 0.799 because it looks close to 0.8 but is actually below the interval.

cognitive_load.justification: Checks interval placement with close decimal distractors.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

question.stem: Which section would 0.7501 belong in on a number line split into sections 0.70-0.75, 0.75-0.80, 0.80-0.85, 0.85-0.90?

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Places 0.7501 in 0.70-0.75 by rounding down too early.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item focused on tight decimal intervals near a section boundary.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers 8.5 by truncating instead of rounding.

cognitive_load.justification: Quick nearest-tenth rounding check from the source examples.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers 24 or 23.92 because the rounding place is not identified correctly.

cognitive_load.justification: Rounds a two-decimal number to one decimal place using the next digit as the decision point.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Answers 6.49 by rounding the second decimal place instead of checking the third.

cognitive_load.justification: Extends rounding from tenths to hundredths using the number-line method.

source.source_file: (PART A) N1-3 Secondary Ready - Applications of Numeracy (FOUNDATION Not Meeting Expected Standard).pptx

misconceptions.diagnostic_signal: Keeps 35.648 by ignoring the fourth decimal place.

cognitive_load.justification: Reteach item that isolates the decision digit when rounding to three decimal places.
